feature-two-column-scene.props: {"headline":"Policy as Code","subhead":"Executable workflow standards","bodyPrimary":"Define guardrails once and enforce them for every human and agent action.","leftRatio":0.4,"rightPanel":"policy-demo"}

{"headline":"Policy as Code","subhead":"Executable workflow standards","bodyPrimary":"Express rules in readable scenarios so teams can define quality bars without writing low-level enforcement code.","bodySecondary":"Policies run automatically and reject invalid transitions with clear feedback, keeping execution consistent across humans and agents.","leftRatio":0.4,"rightPanel":"policy-demo"}

cue.label: Introduction
cue.voice: Define project rules as executable policy. Enforce standards automatically using readable BDD syntax.

cue.label: Readable Specs
cue.voice: Policies are written in plain language with Given, When, Then clauses. Any team member can understand and review them.

cue.label: Enforcement
cue.voice: When a command violates policy, Kanbus blocks the change and returns a clear, actionable error. Rules stay consistent in every environment.

cue.label: Agents and Humans
cue.voice: The same policy engine runs for humans and AI agents, so process quality does not degrade as automation scales.
